title: Social Studies Explorers: Our World & Community
description: A vocabulary pack designed for 3rd-5th grade ELL newcomers, focusing on core social studies concepts related to civics, geography, and culture. Includes vocabulary, a mini-book, and three worksheets.

vocabulary.image_prompt: A diverse group of people standing together, some with speech bubbles showing 'vote' or 'help'
A street scene with diverse people, houses, a school, and a park
A building representing a town hall or capitol, with people discussing
A scroll or document with rules, next to a stop sign or a judge's gavel
Children reading books, playing, and speaking freely, symbolizing freedoms
A child helping with chores, another helping a friend, showing duties
An unfolded paper map showing roads, cities, and landmarks
A child pointing to a physical globe of the Earth
A world map with one continent highlighted, e.g., North America
A world map with one country highlighted, or a national flag
A map of the United States with one state highlighted
A cartoon cityscape with skyscrapers, cars, and people
An open old book, a scroll, or a timeline with historical figures
Diverse children in traditional clothing, or symbols representing different cultures
A family celebrating a holiday or performing a customary activity
A person standing at a podium speaking to a group, or a guide leading others

worksheets.instructions_en: Draw a line from the English word to its Spanish translation.
worksheets.instructions_l2: Dibuja una línea de la palabra en inglés a su traducción en español.

worksheets.instructions_en: Trace the English and Spanish words for each social studies term.
worksheets.instructions_l2: Traza las palabras en inglés y español para cada término de estudios sociales.

worksheets.instructions_en: Color the picture that represents the word and trace the English and Spanish words.
worksheets.instructions_l2: Colorea la imagen que representa la palabra y traza las palabras en inglés y español.

items.image_prompt: An outline of a large landmass, like North America
A simplified map of a country, or a national flag
An outline of a state on a map
A simple drawing of a city skyline
An open old book or a scroll with ancient writing
Children from different backgrounds in traditional attire
A family celebrating a holiday, e.g., sharing a meal
A person guiding a group, or speaking to an audience

mini_book.title: Our World, Our Community / Nuestro Mundo, Nuestra Comunidad

pages.text_en: We live in a **community**. A community is a group of people who live and work together.
pages.text_l2: Vivimos en una **comunidad**. Una comunidad es un grupo de personas que viven y trabajan juntas.

pages.text_en: Every person in a community is a **citizen**. Citizens have **rights** and **responsibilities**.
pages.text_l2: Cada persona en una comunidad es un **ciudadano**. Los ciudadanos tienen **derechos** y **responsabilidades**.

pages.text_en: Our **government** makes **laws** to keep our community safe and fair.
pages.text_l2: Nuestro **gobierno** hace **leyes** para mantener nuestra comunidad segura y justa.

pages.text_en: We can learn about our world using a **map** or a **globe**. A globe is a model of Earth.
pages.text_l2: Podemos aprender sobre nuestro mundo usando un **mapa** o un **globo terráqueo**. Un globo es un modelo de la Tierra.

pages.text_en: Earth has many **continents** and **countries**. Our country has many **states** and **cities**.
pages.text_l2: La Tierra tiene muchos **continentes** y **países**. Nuestro país tiene muchos **estados** y **ciudades**.

pages.text_en: We learn about the past through **history**. We learn about how people live through **culture** and **traditions**.
pages.text_l2: Aprendemos sobre el pasado a través de la **historia**. Aprendemos sobre cómo vive la gente a través de la **cultura** y las **tradiciones**.

pages.text_en: Good **leaders** help guide our communities and countries.
pages.text_l2: Buenos **líderes** ayudan a guiar nuestras comunidades y países.

teacher_notes.objective: Students will be able to identify and understand 16 key social studies vocabulary words in English and Spanish, and use them in context.
teacher_notes.suggested_use: Introduce vocabulary with flashcards and visual aids. Read the mini-book aloud with students, pausing to discuss concepts. Use worksheets for independent practice, small group work, or assessment. Encourage students to create their own sentences using the new vocabulary.
materials_needed: World map or globe (physical or digital)
differentiation_tips: **Support**: Pair students with stronger English speakers. Provide sentence frames for discussions. Use gestures and realia (e.g., a small globe) for concrete examples. Allow students to use a bilingual dictionary.

**Challenge**: Encourage students to write short paragraphs using multiple vocabulary words. Ask them to research a specific country or culture and use the new terms to describe it. Have them identify examples of these concepts in their own lives or local community.
